findings.message: No confirmation step before irreversible delete action. A misclick permanently removes an item with no way to recover.
findings.suggestion: Add a confirmation dialog (e.g. window.confirm or a modal) before issuing the DELETE request.

findings.message: No loading/pending state while the DELETE request is in flight. The button remains active and clickable, risking duplicate requests.
findings.suggestion: Track a loading state (e.g. useState<boolean>) and disable the button while the fetch is pending.

findings.message: No error feedback if the DELETE request fails. The await on fetch resolves even for non-2xx responses, and any network error is silently swallowed; the user sees no indication of failure.
findings.suggestion: Check response.ok after the fetch and display an error message (or re-throw) on failure.

findings.message: window.location.reload() as the success path is a coarse full-page reload. On slow connections the user sees a blank/loading screen with no indication that the delete succeeded before the reload.
findings.suggestion: Update the items state directly (e.g. filter out the deleted item) instead of reloading the page, providing instant feedback.
